title: Deceased Patient Records — Access After Death Date

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.502(f) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.502(f) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.38: Deceased Patient Records — Access After Death Date.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.38' running on index=epic sourcetype=epic:audit, lookup patient_death_index.csv (PAT_ID, death_dttm, restricted_flag), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-90d
| lookup patient_death_index.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT death_dttm, restricted_flag
| where isnotnull(death_dttm) AND _time > strptime(death_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| lookup post_mortem_allowed_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT allowed_role
| where isnull(allowed_role)
| stats count by USER_ID, PAT_ID
| sort - count
description: Monitors chart access after documented death timestamps for users not on approved post-mortem workflows (HIM, organ donation, medical examiner).
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic and sourcetypes epic:audit, epic:audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Refresh death index from ADT feed within SLA; (2) legal guidance on state law vs HIPAA; (3) sensitive dashboard ACLs. Indexes required: `index=epic`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `sourcetype=epic:audit``; Lookups: `patient_death_index`, `patient_death_index.csv`, `post_mortem_allowed_roles`, `post_mortem_allowed_roles.csv`.
visualization: Table (user, patient, events), Single value (restricted accesses). Key fields: `PAT_ID`, `USER_ID`.
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup patient_death_index.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `death_dttm`, `restricted_flag`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Refresh death index from ADT feed within SLA; (2) legal guidance on state law vs HIPAA; (3) sensitive dashboard ACLs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-90d
| lookup patient_death_index.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT death_dttm, restricted_flag
| where isnotnull(death_dttm) AND _time > strptime(death_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| lookup post_mortem_allowed_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT allowed_role
| where isnull(allowed_role)
| stats count by USER_ID, PAT_ID
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Deceased Patient Records — Access After Death Date (§164.502(f) / policy)** — Monitors chart access after documented death timestamps for users not on approved post-mortem workflows (HIM, organ donation, medical examiner).

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup patient_death_index.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `death_dttm`, `restricted_flag`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(death_dttm) AND _time > strptime(death_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(allowed_role)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by USER_ID, PAT_ID** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (user, patient, events), Single value (restricted accesses).
grandmaExplanation: We monitor chart access after documented death timestamps for users not on approved post-mortem workflows (HIM, organ donation, medical examiner). HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
